title: arenaria erinacea subsp. microphylla
intro: Arenaria erinacea subsp. microphylla, also known as the small-flowered sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial with small white flowers that bloom in the summer. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and promoting wound healing. Arenaria erinacea subsp. microphylla is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves to address various health concerns, including skin irritations and infections.
